data.epi_content:
In that moment, thinking something had changed, I lost my reason and blurted out everything—both truths and lies—to Evan.

As if I were someone who had traveled back in time, as if I were someone who already knew what would happen and how he would act.

It was true, but not something I should have said out loud.

"Ugh. I asked him to clean up the glass, but he left without doing it. Oh, did I chase him away?"

I pick up the glass I had thrown at Evan and clean up the shards that scattered everywhere after I screamed and threw it against the wall.

I'm just tired in my own way.

Perhaps Evan has also traveled back in time?

He doesn't really seem like it though.

If he had seen me die, I don't think he would be able to have such a normal conversation with me.

"I don't understand what's going on."

Humming to myself, I search for the pistol in the drawer.

These days, I get depressed if I don't hold it close to my chest.

It's like it's become a part of my body.

Suddenly feeling bored, I removed nine bullets, then placed one bullet in the empty chamber.

And just like before, I spin the cylinder.

After cocking the hammer, since I didn't particularly want to die right now, I pointed the muzzle at my hand and pulled the trigger.

As expected, nothing happens.

Checking inside, it seems I would have had to pull the trigger two more times for the bullet to pierce through my hand.

"Ah. I don't want to die, and I don't want pain either. I'm depressed. Sad.

I'm angry too, wondering why I have to live like this.

It must be punishment. For being cooped up in my room reading novels.

For committing such terrible acts and then forgetting them completely.

Actually, I never forgot, not even once. How could I forget when those memories come flooding back every time I close my eyes?

Though they rarely surface after I've been intimate with this lady, to put it somewhat lewdly.

I just think that maybe the memories buried in my head are getting covered up because it's all too painful."

I felt like I could smoke tobacco now, no matter how much my insides hurt.

"Haha, ha."

I raised the corners of my mouth to smile, then tried to laugh slightly, but my expression immediately fell.

To laugh while telling a story that's impossible to laugh about, you need one of three things: tobacco, alcohol, or drugs.

Anyway, if Evan hasn't returned, what was that change in attitude just now?

It felt like he had somehow found fragments from the future and returned... that kind of ambiguous feeling.

"I've always been ambiguous. That's how I was. Looking at the memories, you were the same.

Later, beyond ambiguity, I became a pitiful bottom-feeder without arms or legs."

Even without turning back time, Evan can change plenty, so why am I in this state?

I've repeated this timeline so many times, yet all I can do is hold a gun and put bullets in the foreheads of people I don't like, people weaker and more vulnerable than me.

"Right, I somehow managed to survive, crawling and struggling at the bottom."

With a cloth in my mouth and a pistol in my one remaining hand.

I just shot anyone. Anything that moved in front of me.

"That was the same whether it was me, or when you were a loser shut up in your room.

It was the same when you had all your limbs too.

Blowing away that little child walking hand in hand with their mother..."

Since I haven't punished myself lately, the lady who threw this useless life at me and always runs away somewhere keeps raising her head.
data.epi_content2:
To punish her, I took an elegantly decorated dagger, carelessly tossed the sheath onto the bed.

Placing the brightly shining blade against my skin, I sliced my arm lengthwise.

The blade entered my arm, creating a perfectly straight wound as if drawn with a ruler.

"Shut up, you useless parasite, that damn red kid was holding a bomb so there was no problem at all of course if I hadn't shot then I would've... Ah, I cut too deep."

Foam bubbled from my arm, and with a hissing sound, the room became a mess.

Blood splattered on my face, and my school uniform was stained red.

"How am I going to clean this up?"

In my anger, and because it had been a while since I'd used a blade, I made a mistake.

Thanks to that, at least this lady has shut her mouth, so I might survive.

"That's what you get.

It's fine! Just as I don't treat those blacks—Satan's byproducts working on farms—as people, I suppose the red people you mention are the same kind.

I understand completely. Haha, as if beasts like that could speak human language."

I looked in the mirror for the first time in a while.

Blood was pouring madly from my arm, and in disbelief, I just stared at the mirror and laughed.

Yes, I should laugh. In this sad world, if I don't laugh, who will?

Was it an auditory hallucination, or words that flowed from my mouth?

I was simply despairing at the fact that I couldn't tell the difference.

Even though I'm weak, miserable, and useless, I had been thinking that I would get a little better, that I would change something going forward, that even if I kept dying painfully, I would someday escape this terrible life on my own, but what is this?

I can't even control my emotions properly, getting swept away like this.

Or maybe my mind is starting to turn in a strange direction.

Let's think positively.

The fact that I'm so swayed by emotions that I can't control myself means my mind is still healthy, right?

Healthy, I don't know.

Maybe "still remaining" would be more accurate.

I'll probably remain like this forever.

Always crying "someday, someday" but never changing, just doing stupid things.

Like cutting my arm like this now.

But Evan changed on his own, though I'm not sure what there was to change.

He's an amazing friend.

I don't know why, but while living in the same timeline, in the same situation, only he changed.

How is that possible?

Perhaps some absolute unknown force is urging me to give up after seeing my struggles.

That friend can change right away without any repetitions, but you, despite dying so many times, despite being given so many chances, can't let go of your petty pride, stubbornness, and obstinacy, and still continue this life without any ability.

That's what it seems to be saying.

So I went into the bathroom, took a quick shower with hot water, pressed a towel from the room firmly against the wound, and wrapped it with a white cloth.

It quickly turned red, but it shouldn't matter since I'll be wearing my school uniform over it.

I dressed neatly, put on my shoes, and walked outside.

Since the conversation with Evan wasn't very long, it was still evening with people walking about in the streets.

I went to the specialty goods store I had visited earlier in the day and bought a container full of cannabis and a box of premium cigars.

Seeing that it contains 12 cigars, I could smoke to my heart's content until I die.

Maybe I'll even die from smoking too much.

No, let's stop talking about dying; it feels too negative.

I just threw 7 gold coins at the store owner and left without taking any change.

Then I headed to the garden to watch the stupid fish in the carp pond, wasting my lungs on this foolish activity.
data.epi_content3:
After crossing my legs, I opened the cigar box, took one out, put it in my mouth, and was about to light it when I heard someone calling me.

"Oh, Miss Erica..?"

"Vivian? You weren't here earlier, what's going on?"

"Ah, no. I was just thinking about what happened earlier, so I stopped by."

"Oh."

It's incredibly awkward.

For me, it feels like it happened almost a month ago, but Vivian just got slapped by me.

Shuddering at the time discrepancy, I ignored her and held the flame to the end of the cigar for a while.

I didn't go out of my way to leave first.

If she's uncomfortable, Vivian will disappear on her own.

I'm fine with being together.

I put the cigar in my mouth and inhaled the smoke.

Like before, an immense pain hit my chest along with coughing.

So I just cried while smoking.

It was similar to the pain I felt when cutting my arm with a knife.

Then, remembering that Vivian was in front of me, I roughly wiped my face with my sleeve.

At times like this, I'm thankful that my face is beautiful enough without makeup.

If I were a student who wore all sorts of makeup, my makeup would have smeared, making me look like a ghost or a panda.

"Vivian, I'm sorry for hitting you earlier.

Could you please leave? I want to be alone."

As soon as I became conscious of another's gaze, I said those words to Vivian.

The feeling suddenly became uncomfortable.

"Ah, eh? What? Oh."

Vivian is surprised, as if she didn't expect me to apologize.

But I'm not the bold, noble, and dignified lady that Vivian knew until just now.

I'm just something pathetic that fears pain and is miserable.

"Vivian, did you not like my apology?"

Slap!

It wasn't the sound of slapping Vivian's cheek.

In that moment, I unconsciously slapped my own cheek.

I felt like if I opened my mouth more, all sorts of nonsense and self-deprecation would come out.

"I'm sorry, it's all because I'm inadequate.

How could there be such an evil person who would slap someone just for holding hands?"

Vivian looks at me with astonished eyes.

I wanted to strongly tell her not to look at me like that, but I just kept my mouth shut and put the smoldering tobacco in my mouth.

"Cough! Ah, my hand, cough! wasn't it."

"No, I'm sorry for rashly taking your hand first!"

"Haha, tha- cough! nks."

My chest is burning up, but why shouldn't my lungs burn too?

Ah, my insides ache bitterly.
